front: Novel: Enid tries to reunite the Lambert family in St. Jude for 'one last Christmas'; husband Alfred has Parkinson's treated with Corecktall
Novel: a character steals an expensive salmon filet by hiding it in his pants and later goes to Lithuania with Gitanas
Novel: Patty Berglund writes an autobiography as therapy and has an affair with Traumatics frontman Richard Katz
Novel: Walter works for the Cerulean Mountain Trust preserving warblers in West Virginia; secretly plans to strip mine coal
Novel: son goes through his poop to find his wedding ring on a trip to Argentina; provides spare parts during the Iraq War
Novel: first volume of A Key to All Mythologies trilogy; the Hildebrandt family in New Prospect, Illinois disintegrates as Russ loses control of his youth group
work: Crossroads and A Key to All Mythologies Trilogy

front: Work: essay distinguishes 'status' vs. 'contract' models of literature; attacks William Gaddis and includes anecdotes about failing to finish The Recognitions and J R
Novel: protagonist nicknamed 'Pip' with $130,000 in student loan debt; Andreas Wolf helps kill a Stasi-informant stepfather
Author: essay 'Why Bother?' (originally 'Perchance to Dream') discusses promoting debut novel The Twenty-Seventh City; collected in How to Be Alone
